calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Continued To Jul-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-14-297845 Conservatorship of Ho Hsiu Shio and
PTR-14-298279 The Ho Hsiu Shio Trust.

Appearances: Lillian Ng appeared by zoom for Andrea Leung (Conservator/Trustee).

The Court asks if there is any development regarding Ms. Stanton. Ms. Ng stated that she has sent letters twice, that the second letter was sent by registered mail with a 13100 Declaration, and that she got the signed receipt card back, but she got no response to the letter and nothing was sent back to her. Ms. Ng also stated that she was informed that Ms. Stanton was 85-years old, that she has been unable to confirm if Ms. Stanton is still alive, that the last that was known is that Ms. Stanton was living with her Son in Tennessee, that she left a voice mail with the Son and that she has not heard back. In addition, Ms. Ng stated that she can amend the petition and ask to have the jewelry distributed/escheated to the State, and that they can pursue fees in the conservatorship, collect fees on the value of the jewelry and terminate the conservatorship of the estate, but they would have to open probate for distribution of the jewelry. Discussion was held as to whether or not assets can be escheated to the State from a conservatorship. The Petition for Termination of the Conservatorship Due to Conservatee's Death, etc., and the Amended Third and Final Report and Account of Successor Trustee, etc. are continued to July 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Ng to have a discussion with Ms. Leung as to how they want to proceed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-30-2020 Continued To Jan-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-14-297845 Conservatorship of Ho Hsiu Shio and
PTR-14-298279 The Ho Hsiu Shio.

Appearances: Lillian Ng appeared by zoom for Andrea Leung (Trustee/Conservator).

The Court stated that it read everything in connection with the two petitions, that it reads things differently than Ms. Ng does, that article 4th of Ms. Shio's will states that all assets pass by the will to the Beneficiary named in the trust and that this is not a pour over will. The Court also stated that this means the assets will go under the will to Ms. Stanton, and that it sees no way around this unless Ms. Ng wants to amend to seek fees in the trust. Ms. Ng stated that her interpretation is that it is a pour over will, that it sees the Court's position, that they filed a request for summary administration and that Ms. Stanton agrees that the expenses of the trust should be paid. Ms. Ng also stated that there are assets in the estate and raised the issue of Ms. Stanton providing a declaration indicating that she is in agreement. The Court spoke about a disclaimer and stated that presumably distribution would then go to Ms. Stanton's heirs. Ms. Ng suggested rather than a disclaimer, Ms. Stanton signing an assignment, stated that she would like to speak with her Client and asked that both matters be continued to see how they will proceed. The Petition to Terminate Conservatorship Due to Conservatee's Death, etc., and the Amended Third and Final Report and Account of Successor Trustee, etc. are continued to January 27, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.


Judge: Joseph M. Quinn Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: N. Ng. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (946 Stockton Street, e, San Francisco, Ca 94108)
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (conservator/trustee) Andrea Leung.

The sale for 100% interest was confirmed at $705,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed 10/6/15. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $715,000.00 is required. The Court stated that the commission amount is $35,250.00.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Authority To Sell The Settlors Real Property And For Approval Of Exclusive Listing Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lilian Ng, for petitioner Andrea Leung.

Responding to the Court's inquiry, Ms. Ng confirmed that the conservatee is currently placed in the least restricted environment appropriate for her. Addressing the necessity of selling the conservatee's former home, Ms. Ng stated that according to the valuation she received the maximum rental value of the property is $2500 per month, and that with the conservatee's projected expenses exceeding $5500 per month at the minimum and a remaining bank balance of only $29,000, the property sale was necessary to provide money for the conservatee's expenses. Ms. Ng further stated that the conservatee is not able to return to her home.
The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Sell Real Property and Execute an Exclusive Listing Agreement (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy K. Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Court Supervision Of Conservatee'S Trust To Confirm Vacancy In Office Of Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-01-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for the Trustee.

Ms. Ng reported that there has been no response from Ms. Stanton. The Court granted the petition for court supervision of conservatee's trust to confirm vacancy in office of trustee (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Court Supervision Of Conservatee'S Trust To Confirm Vacancy In Office Of Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-25-2015 Continued To Mar-30-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Andrea Leung (Conservator).

The Court questioned the status with Ming Stanton. Ms. Ng reported that she has spoken to Ming Stanton's daughter Janice Stanton and that both of them cannot serve as conservator and Andrea Leung was appointed as conservator, that they both live in New York, have health issues and cannot travel. Ms. Ng further stated that she thought attorney Connie Yee represents them and that Ms. Yee would be present at this hearing but she was informed by Ms. Yee that she does not represent them. The Court suggested that they can appear by phone for the next hearing. Ms. Ng will work with Connie Yee's office to make the arrangement for telephonic appearance for Ming and Janice Stanton. The matter is continued to March 30, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy S. Park. (Not Reported)
